instruction: Change the semantics from top-k to bottom-k: instead of returning the k items with the highest primary key values, return the k items with the LOWEST primary key values. The tiebreak_key semantics are preserved (lower tiebreak_key = ranked better, i.e. appears earlier in the returned list). When there is no tiebreak_key, original order breaks ties. Return at most k items; fewer if len(items) < k. If k <= 0, return [].
